wayne-lawler.name: Wayne "Chip" Lawler
wayne-lawler.address: 2659 Cool Lea Camp Road, Alpine, NY 14805
wayne-lawler.notes: Tent space and a dry RV (no shower/toilet in the RV). You are welcome to use the house facilities when the host is home. Garage with basic tools. Email or text is fine.
wayne-lawler.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Days 1–2 (Finger Lakes / NY Thruway approaches). ~35–45 km from I-86 / Corning–Elmira depending on your entry — not on the direct McGraw zip line but close to Finger Lakes wine roads.
Early stop: Weather on the lake belt, late border day, or you want a quieter night before McGraw.
Maps: Full street + ZIP — pin before you ride.

greg-chris-mcgraw.name: Greg & Chris
greg-chris-mcgraw.notes: Tenting/camping; water available; power available; tools/work space; good meal. Best to text Greg’s number.
greg-chris-mcgraw.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Day 1 primary — McGraw is ON your Toronto → Finger Lakes day.
Distance: Destination address — use as your planned night zero in the US after border.
Early stop: Only if you’re hours early — text Greg first (host prefers text).

dan-southampton.name: Dan
dan-southampton.region: New York City area (Southampton, Long Island)
dan-southampton.notes: Access to garage, tools, local info. Confirm full street address and bike parking before arrival.
dan-southampton.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Day 2 (Finger Lakes → NY / LI). Southampton is a town-only pin — not a full street address.
Distance: Large detour from I-90 Thruway if you stay upstate first; natural if you’re sweeping south/east toward NYC that day.
Maps: You must confirm the exact street + bike parking by email before navigating — don’t rely on town-center routing alone.

richard-honchar.name: Richard Honchar
richard-honchar.notes: Spare room, outdoor room, tools and jack for repairs.
richard-honchar.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Days 1–2 alternates. Auburn sits between Syracuse and I-90 Finger Lakes exits — ~40–55 km side trip from a McGraw/I-90 routing depending on path.
Early stop: Backup if Greg’s place isn’t available or you’re delayed near Auburn.
Maps: Full address — verify pin matches house entrance.

mike-miller.name: Mike Miller
mike-miller.notes: Second bedroom with queen-size bed; about an acre of land if you want to camp.
mike-miller.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Days 1–2 alternates. Clyde is just north of I-90 between Rochester and Syracuse (~10–20 km off Thruway exits).
Early stop: Easy bail-out if you’re tired on Day 1–2 and want a shorter last leg vs pushing to McGraw/LI.
Maps: Full address on Dublin Rd — good pin quality.

robert-baldwin.name: Robert
robert-baldwin.region: New York City area (Baldwin, Long Island)
robert-baldwin.notes: Listing had no extra description in your Bunk a Biker export — email to confirm what they offer (suburban home/yard).
robert-baldwin.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Day 2 alternates (Long Island). Baldwin vs “Robert” is the town name — listing may lack a street number.
Distance: Expect ~40–70 km from major NYC crossings depending on route — heavy traffic last miles.
Maps: Open in Maps then confirm full house number and parking with host by email — don’t navigate to a street-only pin blindly.

jay-massapequa.name: Jay
jay-massapequa.notes: Tent trailer use; ~45 min from Manhattan.
jay-massapequa.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Day 2 alternates. North Massapequa is Long Island — same “LI day” bucket as Dan/Robert.
Distance: Far from upstate Thruway; use when your routing already drops to the LI / NYC outer belt.
Early stop: Good if you’re splitting the NYC approach across two shorter days.

genie-cane.name: Genie Cane
genie-cane.notes: Bedroom + bath or driveway. No garage.
genie-cane.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Day 3 alternate (NJ). Cape May is far south — ~150+ km off a direct I-95 toward DC/Luray unless you’re deliberately sweeping the Jersey shore.
Early stop: Only if you’re taking a coastal detour or escaping I-95 traffic with a big southern swing — otherwise burns time/energy.
Maps: Town-level — confirm exact street before committing.

jim-manchester-nj.name: Jim C
jim-manchester-nj.notes: INFO ONLY: may not host — NJ/Shore info & guided runs with lead time. Email preferred; phone checked rarely.
jim-manchester-nj.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Day 3 — info / local rider contact; not assumed overnight.
Use: Shore routing, tolls, and traffic intel — email first (phone rarely checked per profile).

arnab-sinha.name: Arnab Sinha
arnab-sinha.notes: Pickup/tow options; garage 1 bike; guest beds/couch/tent; food & draft beer. No indoor smoking/drugs — family home.
arnab-sinha.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Day 3 (toward Luray). Manassas sits west of DC on I-66 / US-29 approaches — ~80–110 km from Luray depending on route, useful NOVA side stop.
Early stop: Beltway fatigue, storms, or you want a garage/tools night before Shenandoah.
Maps: ZIP-level — confirm exact pin with host; tow/pickup options per profile — clarify expectations.

mark-sabrina-catlett.name: Mark & Sabrina Catlett
mark-sabrina-catlett.notes: Tent/hammock on gravel; business space — respect neighbors. Tools, garage, driveway. Wife +1-270-860-0865.
mark-sabrina-catlett.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Day 3 alternate. Catlett is southwest of DC — useful if you’re on US-17 / I-95 western approaches to Virginia.
Distance: ~50–90 km offset from a straight I-95 to Luray — check live traffic before detouring.
Maps: Full rural address — verify gravel/driveway with a loaded bike.

fred-christie-huger.name: Fred & Christie Huger
fred-christie-huger.notes: Couch, air mattress, acreage camping; hot shower; ~1 mi off I-81.
fred-christie-huger.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Days 3–4 bridge. Fairfield is ~1 mi off I-81 (profile note) — strong “weather / fatigue” stop between Luray and Roanoke.
Distance: ~25–45 km from typical Luray–I-81 links depending on exit — short recovery vs pushing to NF dispersed.
Early stop: Thunderstorms on the ridge or you’re done with interstate droning.
Maps: Town + “~1 mi off I-81” — pin the exact house in Maps the day you ride.

katherine-sharpe.name: Katherine Sharpe
katherine-sharpe.notes: Guest room often available; sunroom/couches/air mattress; tent. Kids & pets. Text preferred. Near Dragon/Smokies.
katherine-sharpe.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Day 6 (Bristol → Smokies → Dragon → Kingston). Sevierville / Chapman Hwy is Smokies gateway — on-path for Gatlinburg/Pigeon Forge traffic.
Distance: Near your Smokies + Dragon day — use if you bail off dispersed camp for a shower night or storm rolls in.
Maps: Full highway address — expect tourist traffic; text host (preferred).

scott-middleton.name: Scott David Middleton
scott-middleton.notes: Garage sometimes; bedroom/couches; kitchen/patio. Heads-up for key if away.
scott-middleton.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Day 6–8 (east TN / west TN). Knoxville is ~35–50 km from Kingston on I-40 — easy same-region backup.
Early stop: Mechanical issue, heat, or you want a garage/couch instead of another NF night.
Maps: ZIP-level city — confirm street pin with host.

shane-mt-juliet.name: Shane (Mt. Juliet)
shane-mt-juliet.notes: Outside space, possible bedroom; covered parking; few mi off I-40 E of Nashville.
shane-mt-juliet.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Days 7–9 (TN / AR via I-40). Mt. Juliet is east of Nashville on I-40 — best when your westbound I-40 day passes Nashville metro.
Distance: ~25–35 km off I-40 east side — don’t use if you’re routing far south/west of Nashville without crossing I-40.
Early stop: Evening traffic, storms, or split Nashville into two days.

daniel-fucella.name: Daniel Fucella
daniel-fucella.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Days 7–9. Clarksville is NW of Nashville — ~80–120 km off a pure I-40 run Memphis–Little Rock unless you dip north.
Early stop: Useful if you reroute for weather, bridge closures, or want a quiet night off the interstate drag.
Maps: Open link uses 173 W Concord Dr + Clarksville, TN 37040 — verify pin matches mailbox/gate at ride time.

harrison-hickok.name: Harrison Hickok
harrison-hickok.notes: Flower farm ~3 ac; dogs/fosters; shared kitchen/bath; RV sometimes; garage/tools/welder. 24h notice preferred. Smoke/alcohol/420 outside only.
harrison-hickok.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Days 7–9. Bartlett is northeast Memphis metro — ~15–35 km from I-40 depending on approach.
Early stop: Heat, fatigue, or mechanical — strong shop/tools profile; 24h notice preferred.
Maps: Full street address — confirm farm gate / dogs with host.

dj-suter.name: DJ Suter
dj-suter.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Days 7–9. Arlington TN is east of Memphis — similar “Memphis metro” bucket as Bartlett; check I-40 vs I-55 routing for your day.
Early stop: Long plains day bailout before Arkansas.
Maps: Town-level — confirm exact road with host (farm property).

jeff-roberts-ozark.name: Jeff Roberts
jeff-roberts-ozark.notes: 3 acres tents; trailers/tools/lift/workshop. Map pin ~1 mi off — confirm directions.
jeff-roberts-ozark.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Day 8 alternate (Parsons → Little Rock). Ozark AR is near your Arkansas push — profile says map pin ~1 mi off; workshop/tools.
Distance: Roughly on-path for central/southern AR approaches — verify driving minutes from your I-40/US-167 choice in Maps.
Early stop: Need lift/tools or a yard night vs pushing to Jacksonville/Little Rock.
Maps: Confirm directions on Philpot Rd — don’t trust pin alone.

brian-cooper.name: Brian Cooper
brian-cooper.notes: Queen bed inside; outside space; power; 1 bike inside building.
brian-cooper.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Day 8 alternate. Van Buren (AR) is Fort Smith metro / I-40 western Arkansas — strong if your routing uses I-40 W toward OK/TX.
Distance: ~25–45 km from I-40 depending on exit — quick motel alternative energy-saver.
Maps: Full McClure Rd address — verify.

connie-jeff-lackey.name: Connie & Jeff Lackey
connie-jeff-lackey.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 1 · Day 8 alternate. Greenwood is south of Fort Smith — similar western AR bucket as Van Buren.
Early stop: Heat or you’re stopping before Little Rock segment.
Maps: Full Evergreen St — verify.

kelly-snyder.name: Kelly Snyder (Route 66 Midpoint)
kelly-snyder.notes: Souvenir shop property; food/drinks donation; BYO alcohol. **24-hour notice via text.** Many riders possible; confirm seasonal hours.
kelly-snyder.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Days 10–11 (Fort Worth → Amarillo → ABQ). Adrian TX midpoint is ON I-40 / Route 66 — your Day 10 primary merged stay.
Distance: At or beside your corridor — minimal extra km if timed right.
Early stop: Wind fatigue on plains — text ahead (24h notice required per profile).

derek-duncan-denver.name: Derek Duncan
derek-duncan-denver.notes: Front/back yard camping; pets OK if traveling with pets.
derek-duncan-denver.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 12 alternate (ABQ → San Juans) — Colorado Front Range is a major detour from I-40/I-25 unless you’re cutting north early.
Distance: Hundreds of km off a direct ABQ → Silverton line — use only for weather closure of mountain passes or intentional rest route.
Maps: Full Hazel Court — Denver metro traffic/heat — plan arrival off-peak.

jim-wendy-conifer.name: Jim & Wendy S
jim-wendy-conifer.notes: 9,100 ft; woods or balcony camping; towel/shower; up to 4 bikers; wildlife; fire/smoking rules — high fire risk area.
jim-wendy-conifer.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 12 alternate. Conifer is Front Range foothills SW of Denver — same “big detour north” caveat as Denver host; 9,100 ft — weather and fatigue matter.
Early stop: Altitude headache or you’re aborting a mountain day — not for saving km on the direct SW route.
Maps: Town-level — confirm driveway/access for heavy bike.

cc-golden.name: CC
cc-golden.notes: Bed, couch, camping, food; 4 dogs; smoking outside; cul-de-sac.
cc-golden.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 12 alternate. Golden CO is also a northern detour from the direct NM→Silverton run — use for social/recovery nights, not corridor efficiency.
Maps: Full Xenophon Ct — residential cul-de-sac; confirm parking.

travis-georgetown.name: Travis Gilbertson
travis-georgetown.notes: King bed + private bath; mountain riding; walk to bars; pet-friendly if pets behave.
travis-georgetown.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 12 alternate. Georgetown (I-70 mountain) — still north of your direct Four Corners line from ABQ — detour logic same as other CO hosts.
Early stop: Pass weather or you want a mountain-town shower night.
Maps: Town only — must confirm address by email.

robert-vandenbroeke.name: Robert VandenBroeke
robert-vandenbroeke.notes: Queen bed basement + sink/toilet. Text/WhatsApp. Jeep tour possible — you provide gas.
robert-vandenbroeke.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Days 15–16 primary (Moab). Moab is ON your Bryce → Arches → rest itinerary.
Distance: Destination town — confirm exact house pin; desert heat — aim for morning arrival.
Early stop: Second night only if host agrees — your plan already uses 2 nights; text/WhatsApp per profile.

good-adv-cortez.name: Good ADV
good-adv-cortez.notes: Tent; tools; tire gear; possible trailer; paid Airbnbs on site optional.
good-adv-cortez.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Days 13–14 alternate (Four Corners). Cortez CO is near US-491 / Mesa Verde side — reasonable if you’re sweeping north from NM toward Utah.
Distance: ~50–80 km off US-163 depending on path — worth it for tools/tire help vs limping into Page.
Maps: Full County Rd G — gravel possible; ADV-oriented.

matt-burton-seligman.name: Matt Burton
matt-burton-seligman.notes: Off-grid S of Seligman; gravel; ADV-oriented; shop/lift; read full BAB profile.
matt-burton-seligman.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Days 11–14 alternate (AZ I-40 / 66). Seligman is ON historic 66 west of Flagstaff — great if your day follows I-40 west.
Distance: Minimal lateral offset if you’re already on 66 — off-grid S of town per profile; confirm gravel.
Early stop: Heat or mechanical on the long AZ crossing.
Maps: Town-level — read full BAB profile before committing.

montana-shaffer.name: Montana Shaffer
montana-shaffer.notes: Home; outside space for trailers or inside.
montana-shaffer.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 18 primary (St. George → Mojave → Apple Valley). Apple Valley is Victorville/Barstow basin — ON I-15 desert approach to LA.
Distance: ~15–35 km from I-15 depending on route — good late-day Mojave arrival.
Maps: Full Wren St — confirm gate/parking.

anais-redlands.name: Anais
anais-redlands.notes: Tent patio or guest room; pool/jacuzzi by arrangement; garage/tools. **Phone call required** to align.
anais-redlands.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 18–19 alternates. Redlands is inland Empire east of LA — use if you’re splitting LA basin across a shorter day before PCH.
Distance: Detour from pure I-15 to Barstow — plan extra time in heat/traffic.
Maps: Town-level — phone call required per profile — align before navigating.

layla-sisney.name: Layla Sisney
layla-sisney.notes: Couch or small backyard; tools/truck/trailer. Advance contact; pet policy; quiet 10p–7a; max 2 nights/2 people; valid ID.
layla-sisney.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 18–19 alternates. Oxnard is coastal Ventura County — natural if you’re already on US-101 / PCH north from LA.
Distance: On-path for coastal routing; heavy traffic possible — arrive before dark.
Maps: Full Diego Way — rules/ID per profile.

darlene-morro.name: Darlene Bennett
darlene-morro.notes: Cottage; driveway; local intel. Limited hosting — text for date fit.
darlene-morro.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Days 19–20 alternates. Morro Bay is ON Hwy 1 central coast — strong if you’re short of Prewitt or fogged out.
Early stop: Bail-out from long Big Sur day — text host (limited hosting).
Maps: Hwy 1 address vague — confirm exact driveway/parking.

jeff-rothman.name: Jeff Rothman
jeff-rothman.notes: Room + couch; garage/tools; wants name/route/bike/social; notice preferred; no pets; M–F away 7–7.
jeff-rothman.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Days 19–21 alternates. Santa Rosa is inland North Bay — ~30–80 km from Hwy 1 depending on your coastal choice.
Early stop: NorCal fog fatigue or you leave the coast for warmth/services.
Maps: Full Hewett St — weekday away hours per profile.

scott-taggart.name: Scott Taggart
scott-taggart.notes: Backyard camping; shop/lift/tire; garage 2 bikes; laundry. **Pin approximate — confirm address.**
scott-taggart.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 21+ alternates. Roseville is Sacramento metro east — useful if you’re cutting inland on US-50/I-80 toward Tahoe/redwoods.
Maps: Pin approximate per profile — must confirm exact Fort Collins Way address.

jim-haines-shasta.name: Jim Haines
jim-haines-shasta.notes: Mt. Shasta area; lawn; bath; tools/trailer; cats.
jim-haines-shasta.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 21–22 (redwoods / N. CA). Edgewood is south of Mt Shasta on I-5 corridor — ON north–south coastal return to OR.
Early stop: Smoke, cold marine layer, or long US-101 day — shop/tools per profile.
Maps: Full Creamery Lane — verify.

lisa-blue-bend.name: Lisa Blue
lisa-blue-bend.notes: Bedroom; gated parking; garage tools/lift; 24h notice + ETA; max 2 nights preferred; LGBTQ+ friendly.
lisa-blue-bend.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 22–24 (Oregon). Bend is central OR — use if your route crosses US-97 (common for Cascades).
Distance: Detour if you’re purely on US-101 coast — hundreds of km — don’t use for “on the way” unless routing inland.
Maps: Town-level — 24h notice per profile.

suze-riley-maupin.name: Suze Riley
suze-riley-maupin.notes: Large yard camping; maybe inside; best odds Sat–Wed.
suze-riley-maupin.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 22–24. Maupin is on US-197 / Deschutes — good if you’re on the east side of the Cascades toward the Gorge.
Early stop: Gorge wind day bailout — Sat–Wed best odds per profile.
Maps: Town-level — confirm.

mosko-moto.name: Mosko Moto / Bates Mototel
mosko-moto.address: 186 East Jewett Blvd, White Salmon, WA 98672
mosko-moto.notes: **Register first** — Mototel N of HQ; gravel access; moto-oriented. Office weekday or IG @batesmototel.
mosko-moto.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 23–24 (Columbia Gorge). White Salmon / Bates Mototel is ON common Portland–Gorge motorcycle routes (WA-14 / Hood River area).
Distance: Near your PNW crossing — register first per profile; gravel access.
Maps: Full Jewett Blvd — office hours for check-in.

david-kishpaugh.name: David Kishpaugh
david-kishpaugh.address: 29820 SE Eagle Creek Road, Estacada, OR 97023
david-kishpaugh.notes: Room/camping; full shop/lift; trailer help. **Phone/text fastest.**
david-kishpaugh.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 23–24. Estacada SE of Portland — ~40–70 km from I-5 depending on path — good tools/trailer help if you’re routing through Portland metro.
Maps: Full Eagle Creek Rd — call/text fastest per profile.

jeff-carrie-bob.name: Jeff and Carrie Bob
jeff-carrie-bob.notes: Camping; garage/tools/lift; pets; often same-day OK.
jeff-carrie-bob.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 23–24. Portland west side — metro traffic; use for rest/tools if you’re stopping in PDX.
Maps: Full Edgewood St — pets OK per profile.

david-rs-washougal.name: David R S
david-rs-washougal.notes: Tent + shop; inside if available. No call after 9pm.
david-rs-washougal.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 23–24. Washougal is east of Vancouver WA — Gorge / Portland outer belt — good last stop before heading east on US-12.
Maps: Full Canyon Creek Rd — no calls after 9pm.

christe-smith-rainier.name: Christe Smith (Stewart Ranch)
christe-smith-rainier.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 24–25. Rainier WA is south toward Mt Rainier — detour from I-5 unless you’re touring the volcano side.
Early stop: If you want rural camp + shop evenings per profile.
Maps: Full Tipsoo Loop — verify.

ob-graham.name: O.B.
ob-graham.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 24–25. Graham WA is south Puget Sound — similar “south of Seattle” bucket; I-5 / SR-512 corridor.
Maps: Full Meridian Ave — verify rural driveway.

bob-combs.name: Bob Combs
bob-combs.notes: Mt Si area; shower; shop lifts. **No pets.**
bob-combs.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 25 (Seattle area). North Bend is I-90 east of Seattle — ON-path if you’re crossing Snoqualmie Pass eastbound same day or next.
Early stop: Pass weather or you want Seattle tools without downtown parking pain.
Maps: Full 464th Ave SE — no pets per profile.

julie-lake-tapps.name: Julie C Cardenas
julie-lake-tapps.address: 3609 Lakeridge Dr E, Lake Tapps, WA 98391
julie-lake-tapps.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 2 · Day 25. Lake Tapps is Auburn/Puyallup south sound — suburban backup if Seattle hosts are full.
Maps: Full Lakeridge Dr — verify.

bill-darrington.name: Bill
bill-darrington.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 3 · Day 25+ (north Cascades return). Darrington is US-2 mountain corridor — strong if you’re on the scenic loop toward Spokane.
Early stop: Rain/wind on North Cascades Hwy — shorter day to recover.
Maps: Town-level — confirm.

dave-katherine-cashmere.name: Dave & Katherine D
dave-katherine-cashmere.notes: Guest room, basement couch, yard camping; laundry; meals if timing works. Text preferred.
dave-katherine-cashmere.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 3 · US-2 / central WA. Cashmere–Wenatchee fruit belt — good inland-heat rest night.
Maps: Town-level — text preferred.

angela-east-wenatchee.name: Angela Parkison
angela-east-wenatchee.notes: Dirt road; rooms or camping; big dogs.
angela-east-wenatchee.routeGuidance: Corridor: Leg 3 · US-2 / Columbia River. East Wenatchee — same region as Cashmere; dirt road per profile — verify bike access.

betty-chewelah.name: Betty (Kenny's place)
betty-chewelah.notes: Posting for brother Kenny — tent; tools.
betty-chewelah.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 3 · northeastern WA toward ID/MT. Chewelah is north of Spokane — use on US-395 / Colville corridor legs.
Maps: Full King Ave — verify.

randy-rathdrum.name: Randy Dominicak
randy-rathdrum.notes: Forest camping. **Text first; call only after text.**
randy-rathdrum.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 3 · northern ID. Rathdrum is north of Coeur d’Alene — ~15–30 km off I-90 depending on route — forest camping per profile.
Maps: Full Wilkinson Rd — text before call per profile.

karl-angie-stevensville.name: Karl & Angie
karl-angie-stevensville.notes: Bitterroot valley; camping common; text full name/FB, group size, dates.
karl-angie-stevensville.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 3 · Bitterroot MT (US-93). Stevensville is S of Missoula — ON common Lolo Pass approaches from the south.
Early stop: Smoke, pass fatigue, or you want Bitterroot valley rest.
Maps: Confirm by text — town in listing.

phyl-corvallis-mt.name: Phyl
phyl-corvallis-mt.notes: Tent pasture/pines; well water; Bitterroot views. Text fastest.
phyl-corvallis-mt.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 3 · Bitterroot MT. Corvallis MT (not OR) — same US-93 valley as Stevensville — alternate in same region.
Maps: Town-level — text fastest.

dustin-patton.name: Dustin Patton
dustin-patton.notes: RV/tent w/ power & water; shop; 1 mi gravel; advance notice.
dustin-patton.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 3 · Missoula area. Frenchtown is W of Missoula — ~10–20 km off I-90 — good gravel-aware stop with power/water per profile.
Maps: Full Twin Lakes Rd — 1 mi gravel per profile.

kevin-island-park.name: Kevin
kevin-island-park.notes: Camping; **1 night max** without prior OK; text preferred.
kevin-island-park.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 3 · ID/MT (Yellowstone approaches). Island Park is west of Yellowstone — ON US-20 if that’s your entry — 1 night max without OK per profile.
Early stop: Yellowstone traffic/smoke bailout — text first.
Maps: Town-level — confirm.

daryn-engesser.name: Daryn Engesser
daryn-engesser.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 3 · northwest WY. Powell is east of Yellowstone — useful Cody/Powell US-14/16 approaches.
Maps: Town-level — verify.

jolene-casper.name: Jolene Beiswenger
jolene-casper.notes: Family + dog; laundry; may cook; flexible timing.
jolene-casper.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 3 · central WY. Casper is I-25 US route — good plains crossing stop if your return uses Wyoming.
Maps: Town only — flexible timing per profile.

michelle-custer.name: Michelle Lamphere
michelle-custer.notes: **Not during Sturgis rally** — tent or possible guest room.
michelle-custer.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 3 · Black Hills SD. Custer is near I-90 / US-16 — NOT during Sturgis rally per profile — check June vs rally dates.
Early stop: Hills fatigue or storm on plains approach.
Maps: Town-level — email.

dave-van-gorkom.name: Dave van Gorkom
dave-van-gorkom.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 3 · western SD. Spearfish is I-90 Black Hills north — good if you’re on northern plains return.
Maps: Full Triple L Loop — verify.

lisa-redfield.name: Lisa
lisa-redfield.notes: Tent; shower/laundry/food; pets inside; smoking outside.
lisa-redfield.routeGuidance:
Corridor: Leg 3 · eastern SD plains. Redfield is off I-29 / US-212 corridor — use for long plains day recovery toward MN.
Maps: Town-level — pets inside per profile — confirm if you have gear animals.
